name: Web snapshot backup packaging
description: Packaged snapshots of a public site tree plus dated archives for hand-offs—data-governance aware: reproducible artifacts, explicit exclusion guidance, and no credential leakage in shared bundles.
longDescription: Versioned zip archives alongside a public_html-style tree so teams can compare, restore, or audit static and PHP deployments. The layout supports disciplined packaging, `.gitignore`-style thinking for large binaries, and clear separation from credentials and internal server paths—treating backups as a security object, not only a recovery object.
features: Dated archive artifacts for point-in-time recovery discussions
Co-located site tree for comparison with packaged outputs
.gitignore patterns for large binaries where appropriate
highlights: Operational maturity around site snapshots and verifiable point-in-time artifacts
Useful for migration, compliance, and audit conversations with stakeholders
Security-conscious packaging: know what must never enter a shared archive
challenges: Backups can accidentally include secrets if entire trees are archived without exclusion rules.
solutions: Document what to exclude (.env, private keys), prefer sanitized exports for sharing, and never publish archive passwords.
security: Keep credentials out of packaged trees
Encrypt offline backups when stored on removable media
